summary: Five distinct, consequential families survive after deduping and honesty checks: downstream-first lane reset, loop ramp, EMA selector on the true score lane, quantization-envelope internalization, and sensitivity-based mixed-bit allocation. Most other candidates are duplicates, blocked by missing primitives, or overclaim current catalog support, but enough strong material remains to justify a real multi-family campaign.
global_warnings: Several candidates claim or imply catalog executability without naming a clearly existing pr1394 harness primitive.
The controller/handoff lines are conceptually strong but belong in a primitive backlog until online signals and bounded transition programs are actually wired into the active harness.
The set contains too many family duplicates; distillation should keep one representative per mechanism class before packing.
feedback_to_generator: Next round should distill to one representative per family group, keep every selector probe downstream-first on quantized_sliding_window score_bpb, and separate blocked controller/handoff primitives from executable-now campaign families instead of mixing them together.

reasons: Real regime-transition story, but the current material does not tie `handoff_receiver` to a clearly runnable pr1394 catalog family.
Without an explicit bounded receiver primitive, this risks hiding a late-patch bundle behind a family label.
rewrite_instructions: Map it to one explicit current harness primitive with named receiver actions and one handoff trigger, or mark it deferred/new machinery.
Make the probe a fixed shared trunk plus one bounded receiver family judged only on downstream `score_bpb` with matched no-handoff controls.

reasons: Artifact-selection is a credible lane, but this version is too vague about whether multi-checkpoint selector search exists now in the harness.
A stronger selector formulation already appears later when narrowed to an explicit selector primitive and downstream-only ranking.
rewrite_instructions: Collapse this to a named executable selector family such as `ema_selector`, or explicitly mark broader checkpoint-portfolio selection as blocked.
Keep training frozen and rank candidate states only by quantized_sliding_window `score_bpb`, with pre-quant used only diagnostically.

reasons: This is one of the strongest mechanism shifts in the set, but it is honestly blocked by missing online-controller machinery.
Round-0 on pr1394 should not spend executable family slots on a primitive that does not yet exist in the active harness.
rewrite_instructions: Move it up to a primitive-backlog/reset item: first prove signal capture, trigger logging, and deterministic replay on the frozen base.
Return with one minimal bounded-controller grammar only after those prerequisites exist.

reasons: This directly attacks the documented harness failure mode of lane mixing and proxy ranking.
It is executable now because it changes the admission and promotion contract rather than requiring new model-side machinery.
Credible family, but it is a near-duplicate of the stronger `loop_ramp` formulation later in the set.
Keeping both would waste ontology slots on the same staged-recurrence story.
The family is real, but the decisive probe says selection is by pre-quant val_bpb, which violates the declared downstream-first objective.
A corrected version exists later in the set and should dominate this one.
rewrite_instructions: Select among EMA states by downstream `score_bpb`, with pre-quant treated only as a diagnostic side channel.
Narrow it to the explicit `ema_selector` primitive and drop the pre-quant winner rule.

reasons: This is a first-order train-to-deploy mismatch family, not a clip-threshold retune.
The falsifier is sharp: either late training improves translation into downstream quantized score or the family retires.
This changes the deployment allocation rule under a fixed budget, which is consequential and lane-clean.
It maps to an explicit executable family and has a clear budget-respecting falsifier.
Selector-lane framing is excellent, but executability is overclaimed unless the harness already supports multi-checkpoint export bakeoffs.
As written it is broader than the catalog evidence supplied in this round.
rewrite_instructions: Reduce it to an explicit existing selector primitive such as `ema_selector`, or mark checkpoint-portfolio selection deferred.
Keep the probe same-run, downstream-only, and anchored by repeated controls.

reasons: Shared-trunk portfolio search is interesting, but it is composition-heavy before solo family evidence exists on this frozen base.
The current material does not demonstrate a clearly runnable branching primitive in the active harness.
rewrite_instructions: Move down one level to a single explicit late-finisher or handoff family before asking for portfolio composition.
Make the first probe one branch point, one branch family, one direct-finish control, and downstream-only selection.

reasons: Strongest conceptual line in the set, but still blocked by missing online-controller integration.
Do not fake executability during a harness-validation round.
rewrite_instructions: Promote this to primitive backlog: integrate stage3_3-style signals into the active harness and verify trigger logging first.
Then re-enter with one minimal bounded controller over one or two actions.

reasons: The reset logic is valuable, but calling it a new primitive is too strict; most of it is a campaign policy rule.
A cleaner executable-now reset version already exists elsewhere in the set.
rewrite_instructions: Rewrite it as an admission policy with explicit control-spread and missing-metric abort conditions.
Do not spend a family slot on automation; spend it on the rule that governs whether search continues.

reasons: This is the cleanest executable recurrence family: distinct mechanism, named primitive, crisp controls.
It breaks a real invariant about abrupt recurrence activation without collapsing into scalar threshold tuning.
In current executable form this still reads as grouped LR and weight-decay retuning around loop onset.
That is within-family exploitation until a stronger regime-program identity is specified.
rewrite_instructions: Move it up from multiplier search to a true phase program with discrete optimizer or handoff modes across the loop boundary.
Make the first probe compare bounded mode identities, not scalar rescaling sweeps.

reasons: This is the cleanest artifact-selection family in the set because the probe is same-run and scored on the true downstream lane.
It is honest about being a selector family rather than a disguised EMA retune.
Strong idea, but it is a near-duplicate of the stronger earlier `sensitivity_bits` formulation.
No need to keep two versions of the same deployment-allocation family.
